articles.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?
articles.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

articles.title: Meta and Amazon are leading a trillion-dollar Big Tech spending spree
articles.description: As the cost of AI infrastructure continues to increase, Morgan Stanley says Big Tech capital expenditures are on track to hit new records.

articles.title: My wife’s 2011 Ford Fiesta was totaled. Should we accept a $2,000 insurance settlement or give up the car for $2,700?
articles.description: “The hood is bent, the radiator is cracked, and the front bumper is destroyed.”

articles.title: Global oil prices top $82 a barrel, heading for biggest jump in 3 months after Trump reimposes Strait of Hormuz blockade
articles.description: The week is kicking off with fresh spikes for oil prices after President Donald Trump reimposed the naval blockage on the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Inflation is primed to fall for the first time in 6 years. Will high prices drop too?
articles.description: A surge in U.S. inflation to a three-year high has begun to recede, but life isn’t going to get more affordable for Americans anytime soon.

articles.title: I’m 67 with a $140,000 pension. Should I wait until 70 to claim Social Security so my wife gets more?
articles.description: “When I pass, all my retirement income is reduced to $30,000 a year.”

articles.title: A hot inflation reading this week could mean a rate hike soon, Fed’s Waller says
articles.description: There is a chance the Federal Reserve will need to raise interest rates soon if inflation readings this week disappoint, Fed governor Christopher Waller said Monday.

articles.title: Billionaires are loading up on these superior stocks. Why it pays to follow their money.

articles.title: Micron and other chip stocks feel the pain of imported volatility — blame SK Hynix
articles.description: The SOX chip index was suffering a unanimous selloff, after SK Hynix’s stock had its worst day in 18 years in South Korea trading.

articles.title: Can big banks’ ‘cheap’ stocks keep rising? We’ll find out when JPMorgan and an unusual number of others report earnings Tuesday.

articles.title: 'Funflation' hits home: Why staying in isn't the cost-saver it used to be
articles.description: Following a wave of price hikes, at-home pastimes like streaming movies or playing video games are pinching pocketbooks.

articles.title: India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
articles.description: Inflation has risen for the eighth straight month in India, as food and energy prices rise due to the Iran war and deficient rainfall.

articles.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era

articles.title: China consumer price growth weakens in June while producer inflation rises to near 4-year high

articles.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics
